title: ranunculus lappaceus var. scapigerus
intro: Ranunculus lappaceus var. scapigerus, commonly known as poison buttercup, is a plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic compounds that can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Ranunculus lappaceus var. scapigerus include soothing skin irritations, reducing muscle spasms, and alleviating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically, but caution is advised due to the plant's toxic properties when ingested in large quantities.
